title: Cyphia volubilis
taxonomy: Cyphia volubilis is a species of plant in the family Campanulaceae. This family is a part of the larger order Asterales. Cyphia volubilis is a flowering plant, and it belongs to the genus Cyphia. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Africa. Its classification as a flowering plant, with its specific characteristics, places it in the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Cyphia volubilis is a flowering plant species in the family Campanulaceae, commonly known as the "Creeping Cyphia" or "Wandering Cyphia". This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyphia volubilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cyphia volubilis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
